title: Procedural Golf Putting
prompt:
Create a single HTML file with a 3D mini-golf putting game using Three.js and GLTFLoader.

## Scene Setup
- Use a perspective camera at position (0, 8, 10) looking at the origin.
- Add ambient light (intensity 0.5) and a directional light from (5, 10, 5) with intensity 0.8 and shadow casting enabled.
- The ground is a flat green rectangle (PlaneGeometry 6 wide x 20 long) representing the putting green, colored #2d8a4e, rotated to be horizontal, centered at origin.
- Place a golf hole (a small black cylinder, radius 0.2, depth 0.05) at position (0, 0.01, -7) on the green.
- Place a golf ball (white sphere, radius 0.1) at starting position (0, 0.1, 5).
- Load the 'MosquitoInAmber.glb' model using GLTFLoader. Scale it to 0.5 and place it at (2, 0, -3) as a decorative obstacle on the course. The ball should bounce off it if it collides (treat it as a bounding sphere of radius 0.6 for collision).
- Add two rectangular wall bumpers (BoxGeometry) along the left and right edges of the green (at x = -3 and x = 3), height 0.3, length 20, depth 0.1, colored brown (#8B4513).

## Aiming & Power System
- Display a directional arrow (a thin cone or line) originating from the ball showing the aim direction.
- Left/Right arrow keys rotate the aim direction. The aim angle starts at 0 (pointing toward the hole, negative Z). Left key increases angle, right key decreases, by 2 degrees per press.
- A power bar is shown as a DOM div element on the left side of the screen, 30px wide, 200px tall, with a fill indicator inside. Power ranges from 0 to 100.
- Up arrow key increases power by 5 (max 100), Down arrow key decreases power by 5 (min 0).
- Spacebar initiates the putt (launches the ball).

## Physics
- When spacebar is pressed and ball is not moving, the ball launches in the aim direction with velocity proportional to power (maxSpeed = 0.3 units/frame at power 100).
- Apply friction: multiply velocity by 0.985 each frame.
- Ball stops when speed < 0.002 (set velocity to zero).
- Ball bounces off left/right walls (reflect X velocity, multiply by 0.7 damping).
- Ball bounces off the MosquitoInAmber obstacle (reflect velocity away from obstacle center, multiply by 0.7).
- If ball goes beyond z < -10 or z > 10, reset ball to start position.

## Scoring & State
- If ball center is within 0.25 units of the hole center (XZ distance), the ball is sunk. Increment score, show 'Hole In!' text on screen for 2 seconds, then reset ball to start.
- Track: strokeCount (increments each putt), score (holes made), par (always 1 per hole), holesPlayed.
- After sinking, increment holesPlayed. After 3 holes, show 'Game Over' overlay with final score.

## HUD
- Top-left: display 'Stroke: N' and 'Score: N/3' as DOM elements with id='strokeDisplay' and id='scoreDisplay'.
- Show 'Power: N%' near the power bar with id='powerDisplay'.
- Add a 'Reset' button with id='resetBtn' that resets the entire game.

## State Management
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
- ballPosition: {x, y, z} - current ball position
- ballVelocity: {x, y, z} - current ball velocity
- aimAngle: number in degrees
- power: number 0-100
- strokeCount: number
- score: number
- holesPlayed: number
- ballMoving: boolean
- gameOver: boolean
- obstacleLoaded: boolean (true once MosquitoInAmber.glb is loaded)
- holeSunk: boolean (true momentarily when ball enters hole)

Update window.__3D_STATE__ every frame in the animation loop.
physics_constraints: Ball velocity decays by friction factor 0.985 per frame. Wall and obstacle collisions reflect velocity with 0.7 damping. Ball stops when speed < 0.002. Ball must stay within green bounds or reset.
